name: Cape Verde Islands
summary: The islands whose Brava Portuguese, with West Indians, formed a sprinkling among Legrasse's prisoners and gave a coloring of voodooism to the heterogeneous cult.
description:
The Cape Verde Islands enter the record through Legrasse's prisoners: most were seamen, and a sprinkling of negroes and mulattoes — largely West Indians or Brava Portuguese from the Cape Verde Islands — gave a coloring of voodooism to the heterogeneous cult.

The islands thus mark one of the far-flung origins from which the swamp worship drew its membership.

sources.quote: Most were seamen, and a sprinkling of negroes and mulattoes, largely West Indians or Brava Portuguese from the Cape Verde Islands, gave a coloring of voodooism to the heterogeneous cult.
sources.context: Chapter 2, The Tale of Inspector Legrasse
